response: The Godfather, directed by Francis Ford Coppola, is primarily set in New York City and Sicily, Italy. Principal photography took place from March 29 to August 6, 1971. The production filmed on location as much as possible, using over 90 distinct locations throughout the city of New York, including the then-notorious Five Points area, the St. Regis Hotel on Fifth Avenue, and the Brill Building. For scenes set in Sicily, filming took place in Savoca and Forza d'Agrò, both near Taormina, as well as Ciminna, a small village in Palermo Province. The film studio initially wanted to shoot The Godfather entirely on location on the East Coast of the United States, but logistical problems led them to use Paramount's Hollywood lot for several scenes. To achieve this, set designers transformed parts of the studio backlot into 1940s-era New York City streets and Sicilian villages. The production faced challenges with shooting in Little Italy due to a real-life mob presence. As a result, some street scenes were filmed in a more controlled environment using Hollywood sets. In terms of its filming locations, The Godfather remains an iconic example of how meticulous location scouting can enhance the authenticity and atmosphere of a film.

The movie's set design contributes significantly to its claustrophobic atmosphere, which heightens the tension among the jurors as they deliberate over a murder case. Although it is not clear where the story is supposed to take place—possibly New York City given Sidney Lumet's background—the film does not rely on external locations but rather focuses intensely on the interpersonal dynamics within this confined space. In contrast to 12 Angry Men, Come and See (1985) was shot primarily in Belarus. The film follows a young boy caught up in the horrors of World War II during the German occupation of Byelorussian SSR (now Belarus). The settings include real villages and forests that were actually affected by the war, contributing to the authenticity of its harrowing depiction of the events.
